ety: fem. of ẕū, q.v.
def: s.f. Possessor, owner, mistress (in these senses used as a prefixed noun; and often better rendered 'having, possessed of, endowed with'); essence, substance, nature, radical constituent; soul; body, person, self (i.e. a man's self, or a thing's self); generation, breed, tribe, caste; genus, species; sort, kind (in these senses, prob. connected, through the Persian, with the S. जाति; H. jāt; which the Hindūstānīs commonly corrupt into ẕāt); (in Gram.) a real substantive, a substantive ( = ism-ě-ẕāt, as opp. to ism-ě-maʻnī, and to ṣifat)
subs: s.m.(?) Inflammation of the lungs, pneumonia, peri-pneumony
s.m. Inflammation in the chest; pleurisy; a collecting of matter in the chest
( = jāt jānā), To lose caste, become an outcast

(apnī), To give up (one's own) caste, or to relinquish the benefits of (one's) religion, by eating, &c. with a person of another caste; — to give caste (to, -ko), to receive (one) as a member of a caste or sect ( = jāt denā)
adj. & s.m. Out of caste; — an outcast
v.t. To put out of caste (see jāt)
s.m.f. An excellent mind or soul; a noble personage; (ironic.) a consummate knave
ẕāt mārnā (-kisī-kī), To deprive of caste (by touching the food, &c. &c. of another)
s.m. (Gram.) A generic noun, a common noun
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذات (zāt), from Arabic ذَات (ḏāt).

ety: for H. जटल; prob. S. जड+Prk. डी = S. ल+ई
def: s.f. Chattering, chatter, foolish prating, nonsense, false oridle stories, quibbling, tattle
subs: s.m. One who talks nonsense; one who tells false and idle stories
zaṭal hāṅknā, v.n. To talk nonsense; to quibble; to tell false and idle stories.

ety: zaṭal, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इक (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. One who talks nonsense, &c. ( = zaṭal-bāz).

ety: rel. n. of ẕāt
def: adj. Essential, substantial, natural, constituent, original, innate, constitutional, real, material, intrinsic, fundamental; personal (cf. aṣlī, and jātī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذَاتِی (zātī), borrowed from Arabic ذَاتِيّ (ḏātiyy), from ذَات (ḏāt, “self”) + ـِيّ (-iyy).

ety: prob. a local corr. of jātīlā = jāt+S. aff. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = ẕātī, q.v. adj. Essential, substantial, natural, constituent, original, innate, constitutional, real, material, intrinsic, fundamental; personal (cf. aṣlī, and jātī).

ety: v.n. fr. زوج; ii زوج 'to couple,' &c.
def: s.m. A pair, couple; fellow, like; spouse, husband
subs: s.m. 'The husband of the virgin or of the godly woman,' a title of the Khalīfa ʻAlī.

ety: for A. روجة, fem. of zauj
def: s.f. A wife, spouse.

ety: for A. زوجية
def: s.f. Marriage-state, marriage; — married woman, wite.

ety: inf. n. of ذاق 'to taste'
def: s.m. Taste, enjoyment, delight, joy, pleasure, voluptuousness
subs: s.m. Pleasure and delight, great pleasure
(se), To have a taste (for), take pleasure (in).
ex: To have a taste in urdu
تمہارا گانے میں اچّھا ذوق رکھنا
You have a good taste in music
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذوق (zawq), from Arabic ذَوْق (ḏawq). Related to ذائقہ (zāyqā).

ety: inf. n. of زور 'to visit'
def: s.m. Visiting (a shrine, &c.), going on a pilgrimage (cf. ziyārat).
wety: From Classical Persian زور (zōr, “strength, force; power, might”).

def: s.m. pl. (of ẓābit̤a, q.v.), Rules, canons, laws, &c.

ety: prob. corr. of A. azwād, pl. of zād, q.v.
def: s.m. Provision for a journey (in P. also zawāda).
s.f. pl. (of زاهرة, fem. of zāhir, act. part. of زهر 'to shine'), Beautiful flowers; flowers; ornaments.
s.m.f. pl. (of زائِده or رائِدة, fem. of zāʼid, q.v.), Things additional, additions, superfluities, redundancies; excrescences; (in Gram.) letters of increase, servile letters.
s.f. pl. (of زاجرة zājirat; see zajr), Exhortations, chiding or restraining speeches; prohibitions, forbidden things

ety: inf. n. of زول 'to pass away,' &c.
def: s.m. Declining (as the sun from the meridian); declination; setting (of the sun, &c.); decline, wane, decay; fall; cessation; defect, deficiency, failure; harm, loss, injury (syn. nuqṣān); humiliation; misery, wretchedness
subs: adj. Liable to decay, perishable, evanescent, transitory, fading
s.m. The time of the sun's decline, the afternoon

def: s.m. pl. (of ẕanab), Tails.

ety: accus. of ẕū; like which it is used
def: adj. Powerful ( = ẕuʼl-qadr)

ety: for A. زاوية, v.n. fr. زوي 'to draw (a thing
def: s.m. A corner, an angle; a retired place, &c.
subs: s.m. Angle of depression; angle on the other side of the base
s.m. An instrument for drawing angles, a protractor
s.m. Angle at the circumference (of a circle)
and zāwiya-ě-musat̤t̤aḥ, s.m. Plane rectilineal angle

ety: intens. n. fr. ذاق, for ذوق 'to taste'
def: s.m. One who tastes much, a great taster, a taster.

ety: intens. n. fr. زور 'to visit'
def: s.m. A frequent visitor, a visitor; a pilgrim.

ety: zāy, rt. of zādan, fr. Zend zan = S. jan+anda = Zend aff. añṭ = S. ant
def: part. Bringing forth, bearing children; — s.f. One who brings forth, a mother.

r: ze, zāʼe mǒhmala, zāʼe manqūt̤a, z., j, roj, roz, jor, zor, abjad
def: (called zāʼe mǒhmala or zāʼe manqūt̤a) is the sixteenth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the thirteenth of the Persian, and the eleventh of the Arabic), and has the sound of the English z. It has no corresponding letter in the Nāgarī alphabet, in which it is represented by ज़, or more commonly changed to ज j, as roj for roz, jor for zor, &c. In reckoning by abjad, it stands for 7; and in almanacs, it represents Saturday, and the sign Scorpio.

s.f. Ornament, elegance, grace, beauty; — part. adj. Adorning, imparting grace (to); becoming, suiting (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jāma-zeb, 'Looking well (or one who looks well) in any dress'
subs: (with acc. of person or member, and abl. of thing), To grace, adorn, ornament, deck, beautify
(-ko), To ornament, adorn; to become, suit, show to advantage
s.f. Ornament and decoration; decoration, ornamentation.

ety: imperf. part. of zebīdan
def: part. adj. Adorning; becoming, befitting; fit, proper; — adorned; arranged; beautiful, elegant, graceful
s.f. Beauty, gracefulness, elegance.
s.f. Adorning, decoration; ornament; elegance
adj. Ornamental, decorative.

ety: imperf. part. of zebīdan
def: part. adj. Adorning, ornamenting, decorating; adorned, elegant, beautiful.

ety: Zend zãtha, rt. zan = S. जन्
def: intj. Good! excellent! bravo! well done! — zěh-dān, s.m. Womb, matrix.

ety: Zend jya; S. ज्या
def: s.f. A bow-string;-border or edge (of anything), parapet (of a well, &c.), a ledge; collar or facing (of a garment); edging, lace, fringe; — the navel.

ety: inf. n. of ذهن 'to be intelligent'
def: s.m. Intellect, intelligence, understanding, sagacity, acuteness, acumen, genius, wit, mind, memory, capacity, ability, ingenuity, skill (cf. ẕakā)
subs: s.m. A subtle or penetrating intellect, a subtle genius
v.n. To set the mind to work, to think, consider
adj. Fixed or settled in the mind, impressed on the mind
v.t. To fix in, or impress on, the mind; to convince (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِهْن (zihn), from Arabic ذِهْن (ḏihn).

ety: rel. n. of ẕěhn, q.v.
def: adj. Of or relating to the ẕěhn or intellect, &c.; intellectual, mental, ideal, subjective.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذهنی (zihnī, zahanī), from Arabic ذِهْنِيّ (ḏihniyy).

ety: az, q.v.+er = Pehl. er = Zend adhairi, fr. adhara = S. अधर
def: adv. Under, below, beneath; — adj. Inferior, lower; — s.m. The Persian term for the short vowel ِ i (so called because it is placed under a letter; the A. syn. is kasra)
subs: s.m. The cloth, or carpet, which is spread under a ḥuqqa
adj. Embarrassed, overburdened with expense, borne down with oppression; — zer-bār karnā, v.t. To embarrass, to put to great expense, to run (one) into debt
To be overburdened with expense, to incur great expense, to be embarrassed, to be thrown into debt
s.f. Burdens, expenses; state of embarrassment; wretchedness
adj. & s.m. Under subjection or command; subdued, vanquished; — one who is under subjection, &c.; a subordinate; a vassal, a subject; (at cards) the player on one's right
adj. 'Under the thigh,' under control, subject; ridden (an animal)
v.t. To bring under, to overpower, conquer, subdue, overthrow, defeat, baffle
zer-lab, adj. & adv. Under the lip, slightly uttered, inarticulate, mumbled; in an under-tone, in a whisper, softly; inarticulately
s.f. Leather or paper, &c. used as a pad for writing upon
adj. 'Under the signet,' under the authority (of, -ke), subject (to), submissive
and zer-o-bālā, adj. Topsy-turvy, higgledy-piggledy, turned upside down, overturned, ruined.

ety: akin to zeb
def: s.m. Ornament (of gems, or gold, or silver), jewels.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زیور (zēwar), related to زیبیدن (zēbīdan).

def: s.m. pl. (of zewar, after the model of the A.), Ornaments, jewels.

ety: = S. जल
def: s.m. Hail; dew, hoar-frost.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ژاله (žāla).

ety: Zend jafra, fr. jap; cf. S. गप्
def: adj. Deep (as a pit, or water, &c.); acute, penetrating.

ety: v.n. of zhāzh-īdan = ćāv˚ = jāv˚; rt. Zend khshu, fr. gaṅh = S. घस्
def: s.m. Idle, trifling, or indecent speech; a kind (of camel's) thistle
subs: s.m. A trifler; one who talks obscenely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ژاژ (žāž)

r: zhe, zāʼe fārsī, zāʼe ʻajamī, z, s, pleasure, azure, j
def: (called zāʼe fārsī, or zāʼe ʻajamī, 'the Persian z'), is the seventeenth letter of the Hindūstānī and the fourteenth of the Persian alphabet. It has the sound of s in the English pleasure, or z in azure. It has no corresponding character in the Devanāgarī and Arabic alphabets. It is often confounded with z, and occasionally changed to j.
s.m. An old patched garment.

ety: = ziyān, q.v.
def: adj. Formidable, terrible fierce, rapacious, raging, angry.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ژیان (žiyān).

ety: perf. part. of zholīdan = sholīdan = shorīdan; rt. Zend khshar = S. क्षर्
def: part. adj. Entangled; mixed, intricate; dishevelled
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ژولیده (žōlīda), past participle of ژولیدن (žōlīdan).

def: s.m. (gen. of ẕū; like which it is used), Possessor, &c. (see ẕū)
subs: adj. & s.m. Possessed of power or authority, empowered, with powers (of a magis trate); — one empowered or in authority, one having competent jurisdiction
adj. & s.m. Possessed of ability or capacity, capable, well-qualified; — a man of capacity or ability
or ẕiʼl-ḥijja, s.m. The last month of the Mohammadan year (on the tenth day of which is the festival of baqar-ʻīd)
adj. Endowed with place or dignity
adj. & s.m. Having a legal or just claim, rightful, just, entitled; — a legal or just claimant
adj. & s.m. Endowed with life, animate, living; — an animal; — adv. While living, during life
adj. Endowed with wisdom or understanding, wise
adj. & s.m. Possessed of rank or distinction; — a man of consequence or distinction
adj. & s.m. Rational; animate; — a rational being; an animal; (as a pl.) animate creatures, the animal kingdom
adj. Possessed of, or conspicuous for, dignity
s.m. The eleventh month of the Mohammadan lunar year
adj. & s.m. Irrational; inanimate; — an inanimate object; inanimate things.

def: conj. = az-bas, q.v.
s.m. Quicksilver.

ety: az-bas, q.v.
def: adv. Sufficiently, in abundance, plentifully

ety: intens. n. fr. زبر 'to chide,' 'to repel,' &c.
def: adj. Strong, very powerful, potent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَبَر (zabar).

ety: for jić, prob. = S. जित, rt. जि
def: part. adj. Defeated, overcome; checkmated; reduced to straits, powerless, helpless; weak, feeble; — teasing, harassing, confounding
subs: v.t. To defeat, overcome; to checkmate
v.n. To be defeated, &c.; to be teased or harassed; to be sick or weary (of, -se).

ety: arabicized from P. zih, 'a bow-string,' q.v.
def: s.m. A builder's string or line, a mason's rule; — a set of astronomical tables; an astronomical almanac.

ety: v.n. fr. ضدّ 'to overcome,' &c.
def: s.f. The contrary (of, -kī), the opposite (of); contrariety, opposition, the spirit of contrariness; persisting, persistence, insistance, perseverance, pertinacity, obstinacy (syn. iṣrār; and haṭ)
subs: (-ko), To be or become possessed of a spirit of opposition; to be persistent; to insist (on); to be or become obstinate
(-kī), To be or become hardened in opposition; to be very persistent or importunate; to be very obstinate
(kisī-kī), adv. In despite (of), out of opposition (to)
(-kī), To harbour a spirit of opposition (to), to harbour spite or resentment (towards); — to be persistent or pertinacious; to importune
(-kī), To be opposed (to); to contradict, to bandy words (with); to spite; — to persist, to persevere; to insist (on); to teaze, worry (for a thing, as a child)
(-kī), To be the contrary or opposite (of); to be opposed (to), be antagonistic (to), be the enemy (of).
ex: موت کے بعد انسان کی تقدیر کے بارے میں بدھ مت کا جو تصور تھا وہ برہمی نظریوں کی عین ضد تھا
The Buddhist concept of the destiny of man after death was completely opposite to Brahmanist theories.
wety: First attested in c. 1665 as Middle Hindi ضد (zid, zidd), borrowed from Classical Persian ضِدّ (zidd), itself from Arabic ضِدّ (ḍidd). Cognate with Punjabi ضِدّ (ẓidd).

ety: obliq. dual of ẓidd
def: s.f. Two things that are totally opposed to each other (as truth and falsehood; east and west, &c.), opposites, &c.; — adj. Contradictory of each other.

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓidd
def: adj. & s.m. (fem. ẓiddan), Contrary; contradictory; persistent, pertinacious; wilful, perverse, dogged; wayward and unmanageable (as a child); — one given to opposition; a wilful or perverse man or child, &c.
wety: From ضِد (zid, “stubbornness”) + ـی (-ī).

def: v.n. = ẓid karnā, q.v.s.v. ẓid.

ety: inf. n. of زفّ 'to conduct a bride to her husband'
def: s.m. Conducting a bride to her husband's house; consummation of a marriage.

ety: for ذهانة, abst. n. fr. ذهن 'to be intelligent'; prob. a post-classical word
def: s.f. Intelligence, sagacity, acuteness, &c. (see ẕěhn).

ety: inf. n. iii of ظہر; see z̤ahr
def: s.m. Pronouncing the words anti ʻalaiya ka-z̤ahri ummī, 'thou art to me like the back of my mother,' a formula of repudiation by which a man is divorced from his wife (a penance is requisite to do away the effect of such a speech).

ety: fr. zěh, q.v.
def: s.m. A bladder; the lower part of the belly; mons veneris

r: zihī, zihe, zahī, zahe, zih, zah
ety: fr. zih, or zah, q.v.
def: intj. How good! admirable! excellent! bravo! well-done! there! lo!

ety: arabicized from P. zih, 'a bow-string,' q.v.
def: s.m. A builder's string or line, a mason's rule; — a set of astronomical tables; an astronomical almanac.

ety: inf. n. of ضيق 'to be or become narrow or strait'
def: s.m. Straitness of mind, mental distress, vexation, anxiety, anguish, affliction, oppression of spirit, melancholy, doubt, anything that tortures the mind, hippishness; difficulty, dilemma
subs: s.m. Difficulty of breathing; the asthma; shortness of breath.

ety: inf. n. of ذکر 'to remember; to mention,' &c.
def: s.m. Remembering, remembrance; memory; commemoration; — mention, telling, relating, relation, recital, report, account; praise, eulogy, fame; the praise and glorification of God (the saying subhānaʼl-lāh, and al-ḥamdǒ-liʼl-lāh, and allāhǒ akbar, and lā ilāha illaʼl-lāhǒ, and other forms of praise); thanking God; prayer, supplication; a reading or reciting of the Qǒrʼān
subs: (-kā), To make mention (of), to mention, tell (of), relate, express, state, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذکر (zakar), from Arabic ذَكَر (ḏakar).

ety: v.n. fr. ضلع 'to incline, decline,' &c.
def: s.m. (but com. f. in Ar.), Side (of a geom. figure, &c.); part; a division (of a province), a district; column (in the page of a book); — the art of speaking with double meaning so that the chain of both senses be uninterrupted through the discourse (syn. talāzum-ě-kalām), a kind of punning
subs: s.f. The division of a province into ẓilaʻs or districts

vulg. ẓileʻ-dār, s.m. An officer who has the charge of the revenues of a ẓilaʻ, superintendent of a district; an officer who makes advances to cultivators and collects rents, &c.; an officer (in the canal-department) who supervises measurements and the distribution of water
vulg. ẓileʻ-dārī, s.f. The office or situation of ẓilaʻ-dār.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضلع (zil', “side, edge”), from Arabic ضِلْع (ḍilʕ, “side, edge, division”). Compare Nepali जिल्ला (jillā) and Marathi जिल्हा (jilhā).

ety: = zīr
def: s.f. A shrill note, acute tone; the treble (in music).

ety: v.n. (fr. ظلّ 'to be or become shady,' &c.
def: s.m. Shade, shadow
subs: or z̤ill-ě-subḥānī, 'The shadow of God'; (met.) a king, an emperor
(or -hum), 'May his (or their) shadow last long, may his (or their) prosperity continue.

ety: inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be easy,' &c.
def: s.m. Ease, easiness, gentleness; tractableness, submissiveness.

ety: for A. ذلة, inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.f. Baseness, meanness, vileness, abjectness, contemptibleness, abasement, humiliation, dishonour, disgrace, indignity, affront, insult; — ẕillat denā (-ko), To dishonour, disgrace, &c. ( = ẕalīl karnā, q.v.)
subs: (-se, or kisī-ke hāth-se), To suffer dishonour, or disgrace, or shame, &c. (at the hands of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِلَّت (zillat), from Arabic ذِلَّة (ḏilla).

def: s.f. incor. for zallat, 'error,' &c., q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِلَّت (zillat), from Arabic ذِلَّة (ḏilla).

def: (v.n. fr. ضمد 'to bind,' &c.), s.f.(m.?) A bandage, fillet, kerchief (bound upon a wound, &c.); dressing, poultice, plaster; ointment; liniment; embrocation.
(v.n. fr. زمّ 'to tie,' &c.), s.f.(m. ?), Nose-rein, nose-string (of a camel, &c.); rein, bridle.

ety: zima+stān; Pārsī dameśtān; Pehl. himśtān; Zend zima+śtāna; S. हिम+स्थान
def: s.m. Winter; — wisdom, science.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زمستان (zamistān).

ety: for A. ذمة ẕimmat, v.n. fr. ذم 'to blame'
def: s.m. Obligation, responsibility, suretyship, security; trust, charge, subjection; burden (as of proof); duty, service, fidelity; lineage; — ẕimme, postpn. In the suretyship or security (of, -ke); in charge (of)
subs: vulg. ẕimme-dār, adj. & s.m. Responsible, answerable, liable; — a responsible person, surety, security; guarantor, trustee, assignee
vulg. ẕimme-dārī, s.f. Engagement, obligation, guarantee; responsibility, liability; trust, charge, custody
(-ke), To give into the charge (of), make over (to), to deliver in trust, to deposit (with)
or ẕimme lenā (apne), To take in charge, undertake the charge (of), take upon oneself, become responsible for, be security or surety for
wety: From Arabic ذِمَّة (ḏimma).

ety: v.n. fr. ضمن 'to be responsible for,' &c.
def: s.m. Cover, fold, or envelope (of a letter); endorsement (of a grant, &c.); clause, section (in legal regulations, &c.); — contents, enclosure, anything included, or comprehended, or contained, or inserted; subject; idea, conception; — obligation, suretiship; — aid, favour
subs: v.t. To enclose; to include, comprehend, comprise, &c.
adv. In the fold (of, -ke), in the contents (of), enclosed in, included in; in the course (of), by the way.

ety: acc. of ẓimn
def: adv. Inclusively, comprehensively, by implication, involved therein; intimately; tacitly; by the bye, by the way; into the bargain; — by way of surety or security; by way of assistance.

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓimn
def: adj. Implicative; collateral, corroborative (as evidence).

ety: Pehl. zīn; Zend zaini
def: s.m. Saddle
subs: s.m. A cloth fastened over the saddle, saddle-cloth, housing
zīn khaiṅćnā (-kā, or -par), To saddle (a horse, &c.)

ety: inf. n. of زني 'to commit fornication,' &c.
def: s.f. Fornication; adultery
subs: s.f. Fornication, adultery, harlotry, illicit intercourse
wety: From Classical Persian زنا (zinā), from Arabic زِنَاء (zināʔ).

ety: prob. akin to S. श्रेणि
def: s.m. Ladder, stairs, steps
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِینَه (zīna).

ety: for A. زينة, v.n. fr. زين 'to adorn'
def: s.f. Ornament, decoration, ornature, embellishment, dress; grace, beauty, elegance
subs: (with acc. of person or member, and abl. of thing), To adorn, beautify, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِینَت (zīnat), borrowed from Arabic زِينَة (zīna). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi زینت (zynt /⁠zīnat⁠/).

ety: fr. zīstan; rt. Zend ji, or jīv; S. जीवन्त, rt. जीव्
def: adj. Living, alive (syn. jītā)
subs: adj. & s.m. lit. 'Live-hearted'; pious, devout, holy; — lively, cheerful, gay, witty; — a devout or holy man, &c.
s.f. Piety; — cheerfulness, liveliness, gaiety; wittiness
v.t. To bring to life, to restore to life, to put life into.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِنْدَه (zinda). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi زندہ (zndh /⁠zinda⁠/).

ety: fr. zinda, q.v.
def: s.f. Life, living; sustenance, livelihood.

ety: zindak = zinda, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Life, living, existence
subs: To be weary or sick of life
ex: زندگی ختم بھی ہو جائے اگر نہ کبھی ختم الفت کا سفر
zindagī xatm bhī ho jāe agar na kabhī xatm ulfat kā safar
Even if life comes to an end, may the journey of love never come to an end.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِنْدَگِی (zindagī). First attested in c. 1625 as Middle Hindi زندگی (zindagī).

ety: Pehl. zand; Zend zañtu; S. जन्तु
def: s.m. Dungeon; prison, gaol.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِنْدَان (zindān). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi زندان (zndan /⁠zindān⁠/).

def: adj. & s.m. Of a prison; imprisoned; — prisoner, captive.

ety: fr. P. zandīk, fr. zand
def: s.m. A magian, fire-worshipper; — one of a bad religion, an infidel, atheist.
s.m. Care, caution; protection, defence, patronage; — intj. Take care! beware! mind! — adv. (followed by neg. na), By no means, on no account; never.
intj. = زنہار zinhār, q.v. s.m. Care, caution; protection, defence, patronage; — intj. Take care! beware! mind! — adv. (followed by neg. na), By no means, on no account; never.
s.f. Fringe, border, edging.

ety: for जिड़ = जड
def: s.f. = zaṭal, q.v. s.f. Chattering, chatter, foolish prating, nonsense, false oridle stories, quibbling, tattle
subs: s.m. One who talks nonsense; one who tells false and idle stories
zaṭal hāṅknā, v.n. To talk nonsense; to quibble; to tell false and idle stories.

ety: = zer, q.v.
def: s.f. A fine, soft, or low sound; acute tone; under-tone; — the smallest string (of a lute, &c.); the treble (in music)
subs: adv. With a soft or low sound, softly
Low and high notes; — a pair of small kettle-drums.

def: s.m. = زيره zīra, q.v. (v.n. fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'), s.m. lit. 'The arm from the elbow to the tip of the middle finger'; a cubit; a yard; — the two bright stars α and β in the heads of Gemini; the seventh mansion of the moon.

ety: = S. जीर
def: s.m. Cummin-seed; — pollen (of a flower)
subs: s.m. Cummin-water, solution of cummin-seeds; a mixture of ground cummin-seed, dry ginger, mint, assafœtida, dried green mangoes, salt, and chillies, dissolved in water (taken as a digestive).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زیره (zīra).

def: (v.n. fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'), s.m. lit. 'The arm from the elbow to the tip of the middle finger'; a cubit; a yard; — the two bright stars α and β in the heads of Gemini; the seventh mansion of the moon.

ety: for A. زراعة, inf. n. of زرع 'to sow,' &c.
def: s.f. Sowing, tilling; tillage, husbandry, agriculture; a sown or cultivated field, sown land; a standing crop
subs: s.m. One whose business or occupation is sowing or tilling, husbandman, agriculturist
(-kī), To till, to cultivate; to grow crops; to reclaim (land).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِرَاعَت (zirā'at), from Arabic زِرَاعَة (zirāʕa).

ety: inf. n. of ضارب, iii of ضرب 'to strike,' &c.
def: s.m. Contending, fighting, fencing; conquering; — (inf. n. of ضرب 'to cover' (a camel)), coitus.

ety: Pārsī zreh; Pehl. zrāe; Zend zrādha, fr zrād = S. ह्राद्
def: s.f. Iron armour made with rings, chain-armour
subs: adj. & s.m. Clothed in chain-armour, mailed; — a man clad in armour

ety: prob. zer or zīr, q.v.+ak = S. अक
def: adj. Acute, shrewd, sharp, quick, smart, quick in understanding, ingenious, intelligent, sagacious, penetrating.
s.f. Acuteness, acumen, quickness, ingenuity, intelligence, discernment, sagacity.

r: zīrā-ki, zī, az, ra, rāʼe?, ki
ety: zī = az+ra = rāʼe?+ki
def: conj. For the reason that, because that, because, since, on account of.
s.m. = zaṭallī, q.v. s.m. One who talks nonsense, &c. ( = zaṭal-bāz).

ety: for A. ذروة, fr. ذرو or ذري 'to be raised,' &c.
def: s.f. Top of a camel's hump; summit (of a mountain, &c.), pinnacle; — much wealth, opulence (cf. s̤arwat); — majesty, pomp.

ety: Pārsī zist; Zend zoizhda, fr. zizhdā, rt. zish; cf. S. जिष्
def: adj. Ugly, unseemly, hideous; deformed; inhuman; obscene, filthy
subs: adj. Of ugly habits; of a wicked disposition; bad-tempered
adj. & s.m. Foul-tongued; — an obscene speaker.

def: s.f. Ugliness; deformity; — evil, ill; wickedness; trouble, sorrow, woe.

ety: v.n. of zīstan = zīvastan = zīv-hastan; rt. Zend and S. jīv+S. अस्+aff. तं
def: s.f. Living, life, existence

ety: v.n. fr. ضوأ 'to give light'
def: s.f. Light; brilliancy, splendour, resplendence.
s.f. = ẓiwā, q.v. s.f. Light; brilliancy, splendour, resplendence.
s.f. contrac. of next, q.v.

ety: for A. زيادة; see ziyādat
def: adj. & adv. More, additional; superfluous, redundant; too much, excessive; — superfluously; excessively, &c.
subs: More would be the extreme limit of respect (i.e. would verge on disrespect, — such is commonly the concluding phrase of a petition in Urdū or Persian)
adj. & s.m. Conceited, too vain of self; — one who has a high opinion of himself; one who engages in an enterprise which he is unable to finish
s.f. The having too high an opinion of self, vanity, self-conceit
adv. Too much, overmuch; at the greatest or highest, at the very most
s.f. Seeking more or too much, inordinate desire; ambition; covetousness; extortion, imposition
v.t. To increase, augment, add (to, -par); (fig.) to take away the dinner and tablecloth (syn. baṛhānā)
v.n. To exceed, be in excess, to abound.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زیاده (ziyāda), from Arabic زِيَادَة (ziyāda).

ety: for A. زيادة, inf. n. of زيد 'to increase,' &c.
def: s.f. Increase, augmentation, addition, surplus, excess; abundance, superfluity, redundancy; excrescence; — excess, force, violence, oppression, tyranny

ety: for A. زيادة, inf. n. of زيد 'to increase,' &c.
def: s.f. Increase, augmentation, addition, surplus, excess; abundance, superfluity, redundancy; excrescence; — excess, force, violence, oppression, tyranny
ex: موٹروے پر پیش آنے والے زیادتی کیس کا حیران اور اور ڈرامائی ڈراپ سین ہو گیا۔
a shocking and dramatic drop in the rape case that occurred on the motorway.
ہمارے کاموں میں جو زیادتیاں ہم سے ہو گئی ہیں اُن سے درگزر فرما
Forgive us for our transgressions which we have committed [in our deeds]
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زیادتی (ziyādatī), from Arabic زِيَادَة (ziyāda). By surface analysis, زِیادَت (ziyādat, “increase”) + ی (ī). First attested in c. 1697 as Middle Hindi زیادتی (zyadty /⁠ziyādatī⁠/).

ety: for A. ضيافة, inf. n. of ضيف 'to become the guest of'
def: s.f. An entertainment, a feast, banquet; hospitality; invitation
subs: (-kī), To give an entertainment or banquet (to), to entertain, to feast, to treat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضِیَافَت (ziyāfat), borrowed from Arabic ضِيَافَة (ḍiyāfa), from ضَافَ (ḍāfa).

ety: Pārsī zyãn; Pehl. ziyān; Zend zyāna, rt. zyā = S. हा; S. हानि = Zend zyāni
def: s.m. Deficiency, loss, damage, detriment; hurt, harm, injury, mischief
subs: adj. & s.m. Doing mischief, mischievous, noxious, hurtful, injurious, destructive; — a mischievous person; a destroyer, &c.
s.f. The doing mischief, occasioning of loss or damage, hurtfulness, mischievousness, injury
(-kā), To cause the harm or injury (of), to harm, hurt, injure, damage.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زیان (ziyān).

ety: for A. زيارة, inf. n. of زور 'to visit'
def: s.f. Visiting (a shrine, &c.), pilgrimage
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زِیَارَت (ziyārat), borrowed from Arabic زِيَارَة (ziyāra).

ety: rel. n. fr. ziyārat
def: s.m. Pilgrim.

ety: v.n. fr. ذعف 'to give poison'
def: s.m. Poison, deadly poison (pl. ذعف ẕǒʻuf).

ety: v.n. fr. زعف 'to kill on the spot'
def: s.m. Speedy or sudden death (by poison).
s.m. pl. (of ẓaʻīf, q.v.), Weak persons, the weak, &c.

(in Arabic) z̤ā (called z̤ā-ě-mǒʻjama or z̤ā-ě-manqūt̤a), the twenty-third letter of the Urdū alphabet (the seventeenth of the Arabic, from which it is taken). It occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic, or in the secondary (Persian and Hindūstānī) formations from such words. By the Arabs it is usually pronounced like a strongly articulated palatal z, or (by some) like ض ẓ; but the Persians and the Mohammadans of Hindūstān give it the sound of ز z. As a numeral it denotes 900.

ety: inf. n. of ضعف 'to be or become weak,' &c.
def: s.m. Weakness of intellect; unsoundness of mind.

ety: v.n. fr. ظہر
def: s.m. The time immediately after the declining of the sun, mid-day, noon, afternoon; — the mid-day or afternoon prayer ( = z̤ǒhr-kī namāz).

ety: for A. زهرة zǒharat, v.n. fr. زهر 'to shine,' &c.
def: s.f. (m. ?), The planet Venus

ety: inf. n. of زعم 'to assert'
def: s.m. Asserting, assertion; thinking, presuming, speaking from belief; — self-assertion; presumption, assurance, arrogance; pride, vanity.

ety: old P. zura; Pehl. zavār; Zend zura, zāvare, rt. zu = S. जु
def: s.m. Strength, power, vigour, virtue; force, strong effort, exertion, strain; stress; weight; violence; coercion; — adv. Vigorously, powerfully; violently, forcibly; extremely, very
subs: (-ko), To acquire strength, to grow strong or stronger
s.f. Power, force; — adv. By force, violently
adv. By the strength of (one's own) arm
v.n. To gain strength, to reach or attain full strength
(-par), Strain to fall (on), to be strained (as the eyes); to be forced, be compelled
(apnā), To display one's strength, or one's power or authority; — to represent a force (in Mechanics)
(apnā), To use force, to use coercion
(-par), To have power (over), to have authority or influence
adv. With force or pressure; with stress or emphasis
(-ko, or -par), To give strength or force (to), to strengthen, impart vigour (to); to lay stress (on), to emphasize; to assist, aid, support; to corroborate, confirm
(-par), To put pressure (on), to press or bear (upon), to coerce, compel
adv. By force, forcibly, violently, severely, heavily, swiftly, grievously, excessively
adv. With great force; with much ado, with great pomp
adj. (f. -ī), Strong, powerful, vigorous; violent, severe; heavy; loud, &c.
(-par), To use force or effort, to exert oneself; to exercise; to use force (against), to compel, to force; — to gather strength, to make head, to be on the increase
s.f. (in Mech.) Moment of a force
(in Mech.) The line (of a diagram) showing the direction of a force
(in Mech.) The point of application of a force
(in Mech.) The line of action of a force
and zor mārnā, v.n. To apply, or to put forth, strength, to use effort; to endeavour, strive hard; to toil, struggle
v.n. To acquire strength, to attain full strength
v.n. To be well exercised, to be in good (physical or muscular) form; to be in (one's) prime
wety: From Classical Persian زور (zōr, “strength, force; power, might”).

def: s.f. Power, force ( = zor, q.v.). s.m. Strength, power, vigour, virtue; force, strong effort, exertion, strain; stress; weight; violence; coercion; — adv. Vigorously, powerfully; violently, forcibly; extremely, very
subs: (-ko), To acquire strength, to grow strong or stronger
s.f. Power, force; — adv. By force, violently
adv. By the strength of (one's own) arm
v.n. To gain strength, to reach or attain full strength
(-par), Strain to fall (on), to be strained (as the eyes); to be forced, be compelled

(apnā), To use force, to use coercion
(-par), To have power (over), to have authority or influence
adv. With force or pressure; with stress or emphasis
(-ko, or -par), To give strength or force (to), to strengthen, impart vigour (to); to lay stress (on), to emphasize; to assist, aid, support; to corroborate, confirm
(-par), To put pressure (on), to press or bear (upon), to coerce, compel
adv. By force, forcibly, violently, severely, heavily, swiftly, grievously, excessively

(-par), To use force or effort, to exert oneself; to exercise; to use force (against), to compel, to force; — to gather strength, to make head, to be on the increase

(in Mech.) The point of application of a force

and zor mārnā, v.n. To apply, or to put forth, strength, to use effort; to endeavour, strive hard; to toil, struggle

ety: zor, q.v.+īn = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Strong, powerful.

s.m. Possessor, owner, lord, master; — adj. Having, possessing, possessed of, endowed with (always used as a prefixed noun, in comp.; and contracted to ẕu, in pronunciation, before the Arabic article; see ẕawī, and ẕī)
subs: adj. & s.m. Four-sided, quadrilateral; — a quadrilateral
adj. Possessed of dignity, or majesty, or glory; majestic, glorious
adj. & s.m. Having wings, winged; — ʻthe winged one,' name of the horse of Husain, son of 'Alī

s.m. 'The possessor (cleaver) of the vertebræ,' name of the celebrated sword of Al-ʻās-bin-Munabbih, an unbeliever who was slain in the battle of Badar; it became the property of Mohammad, and afterwards fell into the possession of his son-in-law ʻAlī
adj. & s.m. Bicornous; — lord or possessor of the two horns (i.e. the East and West), an epithet of Alexander the Great
s.m. 'Possessor of obligations,' an epithet of the Deity
adj. & s.m. Having a tail, tailed; — a comet
adj. & s.m. Acquainted with the sciences, learned, accomplished; — a learned or accomplished person; (ironic.) an astute or artful person, a knowing fellow
(prob. corr. of ẕuʼl-maʻnain), s.m. Pun, joke, double entendre.

ety: v.n. fr. زبد, 'to feed with fresh butter'
def: s.m. Fresh butter; cream; the best (of anything).

ety: for A. زبدة, a piece of fresh butter'
def: s.m. Fresh butter; cream; the best (of anything).

ety: Pehl. zūt; Zend zūta, rt. zu; S. जूतं, rt. जु
def: adj. & adv. Quick, swift; quickly, soon; swiftly; suddenly
subs: adj. Easily grieved or vexed, pettish, touchy; sensitive; irascible
adj. Of quick perception, intelligent; sharp-witted, acute
s.f. Quickness of perception, intelligence, acuteness

def: s.f. Quickness, swiftness, speed, celerity.

ety: prob. fr. Gr. ὕσσωπος; Heb. êsôbh
def: s.f. (m. ?) Hyssop, Hyssopus officinalis.

ety: prob. fr. Gr. ὕσσωπος; Heb. êsôbh
def: s.f. (m. ?) Hyssop, Hyssopus officinalis.

ety: cf. S. अङ्गार
def: s.m. Live coal; charcoal (also written zag̠ār).
s.m. The early part of the forenoon (after sunrise, i.e. the brightest part of the day); breakfast-time
subs: s.f. 'The festival of the victims' ( = ʻīduʼl-aẓḥā), a festival celebrated on the tenth of ẕī-ḥijja, when an animal is sacrificed in commemoration of Abraham's sacrifice of his son Ishmael, according to the Musalmāns.

def: s.m. The early part of the forenoon (after sunrise, i.e. the brightest part of the day); breakfast-time
subs: s.f. 'The festival of the victims' ( = ʻīduʼl-aẓḥā), a festival celebrated on the tenth of ẕī-ḥijja, when an animal is sacrificed in commemoration of Abraham's sacrifice of his son Ishmael, according to the Musalmāns.

def: s.m. The planet Saturn.

ety: inf. n. of زهد 'to abstain from'
def: s.m. Abstinence; continence; devotion
s.m. pl. (of zāhid, q.v.), Religious men, devotees.

ety: inf. n. of ذهل 'to forget'
def: s.m. Forgetting, oblivion; neglecting, leaving (cf. ẕahl).

ety: inf. n. of ظہر 'to appear,' &c.
def: s.m. Appearing, arising, springing up; appearance, manifestation, visibility; coming to pass
subs: v.n. To become apparent or manifest, to appear, to arise, to come to pass, to present itself
v.t. To cause to appear, to render visible, to bring to pass, bring about
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُهُور (zuhūr). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi ظہور (z̤hvr).

ety: corr. fr. A. z̤uhūr
def: s.m. (colloq.) Auspicious appearance, auspiciousness, grace; glory, fame; light; ornament.

ety: v.n. fr. زجّ 'to pierce,' &c.
def: s.m. Glass; a glass, or cup; a glass flask or bottle.

ety: fr. ذکو 'to flame, blaze'
def: s.m. The sun.

ety: v.n. fr. زکم
def: s.m. Catarrh, rheum, defluxion, a cold in the head
subs: v.n. The defluxion from the head to be suddenly stopped; the rheum to become thick and clotted
(-ko), To have or take a cold.

def: s.m. = ẕaḵẖīra, q.v. s.m. A thing hoarded, treasured, or laid up; treasure, hoard; provisions, victuals, stores, munition
subs: v.t. To treasure, store up, lay up.

def: s.m. pl. of ẕakar, q.v.; and see next.

ety: for A. ذکورة ẕukūrat, pl. of ẕakar
def: s.m. Males; the male sex.

ety: prob. the A. dim. of زلخا, fem. of ازلخ; rt. زلخ 'to slip'
def: s.f. Name of Potiphar's wife (whose passion for Joseph is much celebrated in the East, particularly in the elegant Persian poems by Nazāmī and Jāmī).

ety: v.n. fr. زلّ 'to slip'; 'to run,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Cool, cold, sweet, pure, or limpid (water); — pure water, &c.
s.m. The cholic.

ety: said to be deriv. fr. A. zulaf, pl. of zulfat, 'a portion of the first part of the night'; but prob. akin to S. गुल्म, or गुम्फ
def: s.f. A curling lock (hanging down upon the temple or over the ear), side-lock, curl, ringlet, lock, tress; tuft; knot; whisker
subs: s.f. Locks perfumed with ambergris, perfumed locks.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زُلْف (zulf, “hair, specially if dishevelled”). See the Persian entry for more.

ety: zulf+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ; or contrac. fr. P. zulfīn
def: s.f. A sword-knot; — the chain by which a door is fastened; ring (of a door), bolt.

ety: inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).

ety: for A. ظلة, v.n. fr. ظلّ 'to shade,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing that covers, or shades, or protects; covering; a cloud.

ety: inf. n. of ظلم 'to act wrongfully,' &c.
def: s.m. Wrong, wrong-doing, injustice, oppression, tyranny; exaction, extortion; violence, outrage, injury; grievance, hardship; a heavy burden, a heavy assessment
subs: adj. & s.m. Practising tyranny, tyrannical; — one whose business is tyranny, a tyrant, an oppressor
adj. & s.m. Oppressed; — one who is oppressed, a victim of tyranny (syn. maz̤lūm)
(-par), To practise oppression or tyranny (on), to oppress, to tyrannize (over), to do wrong (to), to wrong, injure; to practise extortion or exaction (on)
adj. & s.m. = z̤ulm-shěʻār, and z̤ulm-pesha, q.v.
s.f. 'Dissolving of oppression,' putting an end to oppression or injustice.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُلْم (zulm), borrowed from Arabic ظُلْم (ẓulm).

def: adj. Dark, obscure.

ety: for A. ظلمة z̤ulmat
def: s.f. Darkness, obscurity.

ety: rel. n. fr. z̤ulm
def: adj. Unjust, addicted to tyranny, oppressive, tyrannical.

ety: for A. ظلمية; z̤ulm+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Iniquity, oppression, tyranny.
s.f. (pl. of z̤ulmat), Darkness, regions of darkness; a dark place where the water of immortality is said to be.

ety: for A. زمرة, v.n. fr. زمر 'to pipe,' &c.
def: s.m. Company, body, troop, legion, crowd, multitude, number (of persons).

ety: prob. = S. स्तम्भकः
def: adj. Styptic, astringent (taste or flavour, &c.; — syn. kaselā).

ety: the arabicized form of P. zumrud = Zend aśmareja? or mareja?; S. मरकतः
def: s.m. The emerald.

ety: zumurrud, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Of the colour of an emerald.

ety: prob. fr. Gr. ζωνάρη, or ζωνάριον
def: adj. & s.m. Belted, having on the sacred thread; — one who wears the sacred thread; — a Brāhman
subs: s.f. Investiture with the Brāhmanical thread.

def: s.m. pl. (of ẕamb), Sins, crimes, misdeeds, &c.
s.m. pl. (of z̤ann, q.v.), Thoughts, opinions, &c.

ety: inf. n. of ظنّ; see z̤ann
def: s.m. Thinking, supposing, suspecting.

ety: v.n. fr. زور 'to incline,' &c.
def: s.m. Lie, falsehood, untruth; adulteration; — a false deity, an idol; — hypocrisy, dissimulation, deceit.
wety: From Classical Persian زور (zōr, “strength, force; power, might”).

ety: for A. ذرارة ẕurārat, v.n. fr. ذرّ 'to scatter, sprinkle'
def: s.m. Particles flying or scattered about of anything grinding or pounding.

ety: for A. ذرية, fr. ذرّ 'to scatter, spread,' &c.
def: s.f. Progeny, offspring, children, little ones, young ones, descendants, race, stock, seed.
s.f. pl. (of ẓarb, q.v.), Blows, &c.; modes of speech.
s.m. pl. (of z̤arf), Vessels; — adverbs
subs: China vessels or ware, china, porcelain
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظُرُوف (zurūf), from Arabic ظُرُوف (ẓurūf)

def: s.m. Zedoary, Curcuma zerumbet.
a mistranscription for زيادة ziyādat, q.v.

s.f. Invention, &c. ( = اکت ukat, q.v.). s.f. Speaking, speech, voice, language; story, fabrication, invention, contrivance; inventive power, originality, genius (in these last senses the word is probably derived from the S. udgati; see ugat)
subs: To make up a story, to concoct, fabricate, invent, contrive
To do or perform by means of one's inventive power, to invent, originate, create.

ety: old P. awar; Pehl. apar; Zend upairi; S. उपरि = H. ūpar; par
def: prep. & prefix. On, upon, up, above; at; in, into; with; forth, back, away, against; based upon, according to; on account of. (Prefixed to words it modifies or strengthens the sense, as will appear from the numerous examples which will be found in their proper alphabetical places.)

ety: पैंताना [paiṅtānā, पेंताना peṅtānā [Prk. पायंतअडअओ, or पयंतअडअओ; S. पाद (or पद)+अन्तक+र+कः
def: s.m. The foot part (of a bedstead, tomb, &c. = pāyantī).

ety: caus. of taṛapnā; — ā˚ = āw˚ = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि
def: v.t. To render uneasy or restless; to put (one) in great agitation; to cause to flutter; to cause to stumble, &c. (see taṛapnā).

ety: S. तृष्
def: s.f. Thirst; wish, strong desire ( = tr̤ishā, q.v.).

ety: Prk. मम्मो; S. मर्म
def: s.m. Secret quality; latent power or virtue; power; resources, means; money.
